summary: This cluster investigates thermophilic cyanobacteria from hot springs, specifically examining species such as *Synechococcus bigranulatus*, *S. lividus*, *Gloeocapsa muralis*, and *Phormidium frigidum* in Bakreswar, West Bengal, India. A key finding across the studies was a distinct positive correlation between higher water temperatures and an increased carotenoid-to-chlorophyll-a ratio, indicating a physiological adaptation to thermal stress. The research also assessed the nitrogen-fixing capacity of these cyanobacteria and mapped their distribution patterns in relation to varying water temperature and pH across different spring locations. The geographic scope is narrowly focused on the hot spring ecosystems of West Bengal, India, with an ecological emphasis on how pigment composition and nitrogen metabolism enable survival in extreme thermal environments.
entity_sample: Geothermal Springs of Bakreswar, West Bengal, India
